framework_selection.rationale: Paper is an algorithmic detection pipeline, not a neural network. No deep learning framework needed. pandas covers JSONL ingestion and tabular manipulation; networkx provides graph primitives (union-find via connected_components); scipy provides bootstrap CI utilities; numpy provides vectorized math for score computation. Framework matches the released analyze_sniper_cohorts.py / gen_p7_artifacts.py naming convention.
framework_selection.config_management: YAML via PyYAML + dataclasses (no Hydra — pipeline is simple enough that a single config.yaml with a typed dataclass is sufficient)

files.role: Package init; exports public API symbols
Reads pumpfun_buyers.jsonl and pumpfun_launches.jsonl into typed DataFrames
files.notes: PassiveSolanaObserver itself (live Solana listener) is out of scope for reproducibility repo — we consume its outputs.

files.role: IO subpackage init
Stage 1 — IntraLaunchExtractor. Filters buyer events to first-N buyers per mint.
files.output_schema: {mint: str, wallet: str, rank: int, block_time: int, sol_committed: float}

files.role: Builds weighted co-occurrence graph G=(V,E) and applies edge-weight filter
Surfaces connected components via union-find; applies size filter
files.implementation_note: Use networkx.connected_components() which internally uses BFS/union-find. Equivalent to manual union-find with path-compression.

files.role: Computes EQ1 cohort score and assigns tier labels
public_methods: score(cohort_wallets: frozenset, intra_index: pd.DataFrame, touch_threshold: int = 1) -> dict
classify_tier(row: pd.Series, premium_min_launches: int = 20, high_min_launches: int = 10, high_min_score: float = 100.0) -> str

confidence_notes.tau: ASSUMED: tau ~ 40-50 (confidence 0.55). Implemented as configurable param with default=40.0. TODO: verify against paper author.
confidence_notes.touch_indicator: ASSUMED: uses >=1 wallet for score, >=2 for causal analysis (confidence 0.68). Exposed as config param touch_threshold_score.

files.role: Detection subpackage init; exposes detect_cohorts() pipeline function
Orchestrates the full Stage1→Stage2 detection pipeline as a single callable

files.role: Constructs treated and control samples for random-matched causal design
public_methods: build_treated(cohorts_df: pd.DataFrame, intra_index: pd.DataFrame, touch_threshold: int = 2) -> list[str]

attach_outcomes(mints: list[str], buyers_df: pd.DataFrame, launches_df: pd.DataFrame, window_minutes: int = 30) -> pd.DataFrame

files.role: Computes percentage lift (EQ2) and bootstrap CIs
public_methods: bootstrap_ci(treated_outcomes: pd.Series, control_outcomes: pd.Series, n_iter: int = 1000, ci_level: float = 0.95, seed: int = 42) -> tuple[float, float]
estimate(treated_df: pd.DataFrame, control_df: pd.DataFrame, outcome_col: str) -> dict

files.role: Constructs both placebo designs (uniform-random and activity-matched)
public_methods: UniformRandomPlacebo.build(cohorts_df: pd.DataFrame, buyers_df: pd.DataFrame, seed: int = 42) -> pd.DataFrame
ActivityMatchedPlacebo.build(cohorts_df: pd.DataFrame, buyers_df: pd.DataFrame, match_tolerance: int = 100, seed: int = 42) -> pd.DataFrame

Produces figures matching Fig 1 (size distribution), Fig 2 (Lorenz), Fig 3 (score vs launches)
Analysis subpackage init
Typed configuration dataclasses loaded from config.yaml
JSONL streaming utilities, address anonymization (first4+last4), output serialization
Utils subpackage init

pipeline_stages.name: INGEST
pipeline_stages.input_schema: buyers: {mint: str, wallet: str, slot: int, blockTime: int, sol_in: float, tx_sig: str, rank: int}
pipeline_stages.output: buyers_df: DataFrame[N_buyers x 7 cols], launches_df: DataFrame[N_launches x 9 cols]
pseudocode: stream pumpfun_buyers.jsonl line-by-line
parse each JSON record into dict
accumulate into DataFrame, cast dtypes (blockTime→int64, sol_in→float64, rank→int32)
repeat for pumpfun_launches.jsonl
return (buyers_df, launches_df)

pipeline_stages.name: STAGE1_EXTRACT
pipeline_stages.output: intra_index: DataFrame[~1,660,980 x 5] (up to 10 rows per qualifying mint)
pseudocode: filter buyers_df to rows where rank <= window_size (default 10)
group by mint, keep only mints with at least 1 buyer in [1, window_size]
result columns: {mint, wallet, rank, block_time, sol_committed}
qualifying_mints = unique mints in result (paper: 166,098)

pipeline_stages.name: GRAPH_BUILD
pseudocode: group intra_index by mint → get list of wallets per mint
for each mint, generate all (wallet_i, wallet_j) unordered pairs where i != j
for each pair (u, v): if edge exists increment weight by 1, else add edge with weight=1
result: G_raw with |V| = unique wallet addresses, |E| = all co-occurrence pairs

pipeline_stages.name: EDGE_FILTER
pipeline_stages.output: G_filtered: nx.Graph (only edges with weight >= min_weight=3)
pseudocode: iterate over edges of G_raw
retain edge (u, v, w) if w >= min_weight
paper reports 9,788 qualifying pairs at cutoff=3

pipeline_stages.name: UNION_FIND
pseudocode: call nx.connected_components(G_filtered)
collect each component as frozenset of wallet address strings
result: list of frozensets (each frozenset = one candidate cohort)

pipeline_stages.name: SIZE_FILTER
pseudocode: discard any component with len(component) > max_cohort_size (default 12)
paper: reduces 1,161 → 1,012

pipeline_stages.name: SCORE_AND_TIER
pseudocode: for each component C in components_filtered:
touched_launches = mints where any wallet in C appears in intra_index (using touch_threshold_score=1)
n_launches = len(touched_launches)
for each touched launch: record min rank attained by any C wallet
mean_first_rank = mean of per-launch min ranks
total_sol = sum of sol_committed for all C wallets across touched launches
score = 10 * n_launches + 5 / mean_first_rank + sqrt(total_sol)
tier = 'premium' if n_launches>=20 else 'high' if (n_launches>=10 or score>=100) else 'standard'
apply score threshold tau (default 40.0) to filter out very-low-quality components
assign cohort_id = 'COH-{NNNN}' sorted by descending score
return cohorts_df

pipeline_stages.name: CAUSAL_SAMPLE
pipeline_stages.input: cohorts_df: DataFrame, buyers_df: DataFrame, launches_df: DataFrame
pipeline_stages.output: treated_df: DataFrame[5,411 x outcome_cols], control_df: DataFrame[16,233 x outcome_cols]
pseudocode: treated_mints = mints where >=2 cohort wallets appear in first-10 buyers (strict threshold)
untouched_mints = qualifying_mints - treated_mints
control_mints = random sample of untouched_mints, size=3*len(treated_mints), seed=42
for each mint in treated + control:
compute first_30min_buyer_count (distinct buyers within 30min of launch created_timestamp)
compute first_30min_sol_inflow (sum sol_in within 30min)
compute total_buyer_count
return treated_df, control_df

pipeline_stages.name: LIFT_ESTIMATE
pipeline_stages.input: treated_df: DataFrame, control_df: DataFrame, outcome_col: str
pipeline_stages.output: result: {point_estimate: float, ci_lower: float, ci_upper: float, treated_mean: float, control_mean: float}
pseudocode: treated_mean = mean(treated_df[outcome_col])
control_mean = mean(control_df[outcome_col])
point_lift = (treated_mean - control_mean) / control_mean * 100
bootstrap_lifts = []
for i in range(n_iter=1000):
t_sample = treated_df[outcome_col].sample(frac=1.0, replace=True, random_state=seed+i)
c_sample = control_df[outcome_col].sample(frac=1.0, replace=True, random_state=seed+i+10000)
bootstrap_lifts.append((mean(t_sample) - mean(c_sample)) / mean(c_sample) * 100)
ci_lower = percentile(bootstrap_lifts, 2.5)
ci_upper = percentile(bootstrap_lifts, 97.5)
return {point_estimate, ci_lower, ci_upper, treated_mean, control_mean}

pipeline_stages.name: PLACEBO_ACTIVITY_MATCHED
pipeline_stages.output: placebo_cohorts_df: DataFrame (1,012 placebo cohorts, same schema as cohorts_df)
pseudocode: non_cohort_wallets = all wallets in buyers_df NOT in any real cohort
compute per-wallet launch count for all non_cohort_wallets
for each real cohort C with wallets w1..wk:
for each wi: get wi's launch_count
for each wi: sample one non_cohort_wallet with launch_count within ±match_tolerance (default 100) of wi's count
if match found: form placebo_cohort of size k from sampled wallets
collect 1,012 placebo cohorts
compute placebo-touched mints (>=2 placebo wallets in first-10 buyers of same mint)
return placebo_cohorts_df with same schema

detection.score_tau: 40.0  # ASSUMED: exact tau undisclosed (SIR confidence 0.55). Adjust to match target cohort count.
detection.touch_threshold_score: 1  # ASSUMED: >=1 for score computation (SIR confidence 0.68). Set to 2 to match causal analysis threshold.

dependency_rationale.pandas: Core tabular data structure for buyer_event and launch_metadata DataFrames
dependency_rationale.numpy: Vectorized score computation and bootstrap percentile CI
dependency_rationale.networkx: Graph construction, edge filtering, connected_components (union-find equivalent)
dependency_rationale.scipy: Statistical utilities (not strictly required but useful for future PSM extension)
dependency_rationale.matplotlib: Figures 1-3 reproduction (size distribution bar, Lorenz curve, score scatter)
dependency_rationale.orjson: Fast JSONL parsing (10-30x faster than stdlib json for large files)

entrypoints.purpose: Run the full two-stage detection pipeline; outputs cohort catalogue JSONL
Load cohort catalogue and produce descriptive statistics + all three figures

cli_args.help: Output directory for figures and tables
Number of top cohorts in Table 1

entrypoints.purpose: Run causal buyer-flow analysis: random-matched design + both placebo checks + robustness checks
Master script: runs detect → analyze → causal in sequence, reproducing all paper artifacts
outputs: All results/ artifacts from detect.py + analyze.py + causal.py

docker_spec.rationale: No GPU needed; slim base keeps image small (~150MB vs 3GB+ for CUDA variants)

risk_assessment.description: The exact numeric value of tau is not disclosed in the paper. Our default of 40.0 is an assumption. Running with a different tau will yield a different cohort count (more or fewer than 1,012), making exact reproduction of Table 3 headline numbers impossible without calibration.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose tau as a CLI argument. Add a --calibrate flag to binary-search tau until cohort_count == target (default 1012). Document clearly in README. Use abstract base class pattern in CohortScorer so tau logic is easily swapped.
risk_assessment.architecture_response: CohortScorer.apply_score_threshold() is a single isolated method. The calibrate() classmethod will implement binary search.

risk_assessment.description: Whether the score formula's 1{C touches L} uses >=1 or >=2 cohort wallets is ambiguous (SIR confidence 0.68). The choice affects n_launches_hit per cohort and therefore tier classification.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose touch_threshold_score as a config parameter (default 1). Add TODO comment in scorer.py. Output both variants when --ablation is passed.
risk_assessment.architecture_response: touch_threshold_score is a required parameter in CohortScorer.score() method signature, not a hardcoded constant.

risk_assessment.description: The activity-matched placebo yields only 173 treated mints (vs 5,411 for real cohorts) when using the >=2 wallet strict touch threshold. This makes the placebo CI much wider, and the comparison is power-asymmetric. We cannot reproduce the exact 173 without the exact non-cohort wallet pool.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement placebo with the same >=2 strict threshold as paper. Report sample sizes in output summary. Add --placebo-touch-threshold param to allow >=1 variant for comparison. Note power asymmetry in causal_buyer_flow_summary.txt output.

risk_assessment.description: Buyer rank is assigned by sorting blockTime. Ties within the same blockTime are not specified. Different tie-breaking strategies can shift ranks near position 10, affecting which wallets are included in the first-buyer window.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default tie-breaking: sort by (blockTime ASC, tx_sig ASC) — lexicographic on transaction signature, which provides a deterministic canonical order matching typical Solana indexer behavior. Flag as TODO in loader.py.
risk_assessment.architecture_response: DataLoader.load_buyers() applies sort_values(['blockTime', 'tx_sig']) before assigning rank. Tie-breaking column is configurable.

risk_assessment.description: With 166,098 mints and up to 10 buyers each, the maximum number of wallet pairs generated is C(10,2) × 166,098 ≈ 7.5M. This fits comfortably in RAM (< 1GB), but the raw graph before filtering may be large.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Stream pair generation using itertools.combinations per mint. Use a defaultdict(int) counter for edge weights rather than building the full networkx graph before filtering. Only pass filtered edges to networkx.
risk_assessment.architecture_response: CoOccurrenceGraph.build() uses streaming pair accumulation. networkx graph is constructed only from G_filtered edges.

risk_assessment.description: The raw input files are not in the Zenodo data release (too large). Users must either collect their own data via a Solana RPC listener or obtain from the author. Reproducibility therefore requires data access.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Include a data/README.md explaining how to collect data from Solana RPC. Provide a data/sample_buyers.jsonl (first 10,000 rows of anonymized structure) for pipeline smoke-testing. The released sniper_cohorts_intra.jsonl.gz can serve as a Stage-1 checkpoint to skip raw ingestion.
risk_assessment.architecture_response: loader.py supports reading from sniper_cohorts_intra.jsonl.gz directly to bypass Stage 1 (--from-intra flag in detect.py).

unit_tests: test_extractor.py — verify first-10-buyer filtering on synthetic 20-buyer DataFrame
test_graph.py — verify edge weight computation on known co-occurrence fixtures
test_union_find.py — verify connected components on a 3-wallet triangle graph
test_scorer.py — verify EQ1 score computation against COH-0001 known values (9 wallets, 42 launches, score 430.44)
test_estimator.py — verify EQ2 lift and bootstrap CI on synthetic treated/control arrays
test_placebo.py — verify activity-matched wallet sampling within tolerance
integration_tests: test_pipeline_smoke.py — run full detect pipeline on 1,000-row synthetic buyers fixture; verify output schema
test_causal_smoke.py — run causal pipeline on small synthetic cohort set
regression_benchmarks: With released sniper_cohorts_intra.jsonl.gz as input: assert cohort_count == 1012 (or within ±50 given tau ambiguity)
